title: Benjamin Schatz - Foster Global
content:
Benjamin Schatz is partner at Foster LLP in our Houston office. With nearly two decades of experience, Benjamin has represented individuals and businesses of all sizes in their immigrant and nonimmigrant processes. He has ample experience in a wide variety of immigration matters – from straightforward family-based processes to highly-complex situations involving novel immigration-related issues and federal litigation. This varied experience gives Benjamin the ability to develop effective immigration strategies for his clients and to drive them to conclusion. Currently, his practice is primarily focused on representing foreign national investors and entrepreneurs, as well as multinational corporations.
Benjamin graduated cum laude from Southern Methodist University Dedman School of Law and received both a Bachelor of Business Administration and a Master in Professional Accounting from The University of Texas at Austin. Prior to receiving his law degree and joining the firm, Benjamin gained valuable business experience as an auditor for one of the “Big Four” international accounting firms.
Benjamin has also represented both asylum-seekers and Special Immigrant Juveniles on a pro bono basis, and he previously coordinated the immigration program of a major San Antonio non-profit organization. He has also participated in numerous free legal clinics and naturalization workshops and has volunteered for immigration-focused call-in programs.
Benjamin is licensed to practice in the state of Texas and is an active member of the American Immigration Lawyers Association (AILA) and State Bar of Texas. He is a frequent speaker on immigration-related topics.
Why Did You Decide To Become An Immigration Attorney?
I believe that one of the great strengths of the United States is the dynamism and diversity that comes from being a country comprised largely of immigrants. I decided to become an immigration attorney to help continue this country’s rich tradition of absorbing ideas, cultures, knowledge, and economic contributions from around the world. – Benjamin Schatz
